{"step_by_step_thinking": "Dystrophic calcification is a process where calcium salts deposit in damaged or dead tissues, such as in areas of necrosis or inflammation. Myositis ossificans is a condition where bone tissue forms within skeletal muscles, which fits the description of dystrophic calcification. The other options (hyperparathyroidism, sarcoidosis, and hypervitaminosis D) are associated with metastatic calcification, which involves the deposition of calcium salts in living tissues due to elevated serum calcium or phosphorus levels.", "answer_choice": "D"}
